mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a direct measure of broad economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market is a cornerstone of economic expansions.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds the Federal Reserve's implicit target, it forces restrictive monetary policy, increasing the discount rate for equities and compressing valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, indicating the market is ignoring inflation risk.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, characterized by simultaneously rising unemployment (UNRATE) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL), severely damages corporate profitability and consumer sentiment, causing a sharp decline in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was poor for equities.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive median 60-day return during periods where the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown. This effect is amplified during a monetary tightening cycle, as higher rates choke off credit-sensitive sectors, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 1990 and 2001 recessions.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active during a period of rising FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity market performance over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately 2/3 of GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between real RSAFS 3-month growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the goods-producing sector of the economy, often preceding a wider recession and causing investors to de-risk, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating committee uses INDPRO as a key indicator.
failure_condition: In the 6 months following a trigger event, the S&P 500 total return is positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at a restrictive level, the high risk-free rate makes equities less attractive on a relative basis and slows economic activity, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%, the average 5-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically different from periods when it is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An overheating labor market, identified by very low unemployment (UNRATE) combined with still-strong payroll growth (PAYEMS), signals peak economic momentum and raises fears of wage-price spirals and aggressive Fed tightening, negatively impacting forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Economic peaks in 2000 and late 2006 exhibited these characteristics.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of this combined signal remains below 0.02 in a walk-forward test over a full business cycle.
